volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Two Bacchises (Section 4.7.825 - Section 4.8.882)

sections.label: Section 4.7.825
sections.translation: I , give it you ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.825b
sections.original: Atque orabis me quidem ultro ut auferam ,
sections.translation: You ’ ll be entreating me , too , of your own accord to receive it , when you shall come to know this accuser of mine , in how great danger and in what a dreadful situation he is . Then will you be offering his liberty to Chrysalus ; but I certainly shan ’ t accept it .

sections.label: Section 4.7.829b
sections.translation: Tell me , source of mischief ,

sections.label: Section 4.7.830
sections.original: dic , quo in periclo est meus Mnesilochus filius ?
sections.translation: tell me , in what danger is my son Mnesilochus .

sections.label: Section 4.7.831
sections.original: Sequere hac me , faxo iam scies .
sections.translation: Follow me this way ; I ’ ll soon let you know .

sections.label: Section 4.7.831b
sections.translation: Where on earth shall I follow you ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.832
sections.translation: Only three steps .

sections.label: Section 4.7.832b
sections.translation: Aye , ten even .

sections.label: Section 4.7.832c
sections.translation: Come , then , Artamo , do you open you this door out a very little way ; softly , don ’ t make it creak . ( The door of the house of BACCHIS is opened .) That ’ s enough . Now , step you hither . ( To NICOBULUS , who looks in .) Do you see the entertainment ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.835
sections.translation: I see Pistoclerus and Bacchis right opposite .

sections.label: Section 4.7.836
sections.original: Qui sunt in lecto illo altero ?
sections.translation: Who are upon that other couch ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.836b
sections.translation: Wretch that I am , I ’ m undone .

sections.label: Section 4.7.837
sections.translation: Do you recognize that person ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.837b
sections.translation: I do recognize him .

sections.label: Section 4.7.837c
sections.translation: Now tell me , if you please , does that woman seem of handsome appearance ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.838b
sections.translation: Very much so .

sections.label: Section 4.7.839
sections.translation: Well , do you take her to be a courtesan ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.839b
sections.translation: Why not ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.840
sections.translation: You are mistaken .

sections.label: Section 4.7.840b
sections.translation: Who is she then , prithee ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.840c
sections.translation: You ’ ll find out from me . indeed , you ’ ll get no more information to-day .

sections.label: Section 4.8.842
sections.translation: Is this Mnesilochus , the son of Nicobulus , by force to detain my own mistress ? What proceeding is this ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.844
sections.translation: Who ’ s this ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.844b
sections.original: Per tempus hic venit miles mihi .
sections.translation: This Captain ’ s opportunely come for me .

sections.label: Section 4.8.845
sections.original: Non me arbitratur militem , sed mulierem ,
sections.translation: He doesn ’ t consider that I am a soldier , but a woman , who cannot defend myself and mine . But may neither Bellona nor Mars ever put confidence in me , if I don ’ t put him out of life , if I meet him , or if I don ’ t make him lose possession of his existence .

sections.label: Section 4.8.850
sections.original: Chrysale , quis ille est qui minitatur filio ?
sections.translation: Chrysalus , who ’ s this that ’ s threatening my son ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.851
sections.original: Vir hic ést illius mulieris quacum accubat .
sections.translation: This is the husband of that woman with whom he is now reclining .

sections.label: Section 4.8.852
sections.translation: What ! The husband ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.852b
sections.translation: The husband , I say .

sections.label: Section 4.8.852c
sections.translation: Prithee , is she married then ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.853
sections.translation: You ’ ll know before very long .

sections.label: Section 4.8.853b
sections.translation: Wretch that I am ; I ’ m utterly undone .

sections.label: Section 4.8.854
sections.original: Quid nunc ? scelestus tibi videtur Chrysalus ?
sections.translation: How now ? Does Chrysalus seem such a villain to you ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.855
sections.original: age nunc vincito me , auscultato filio .
sections.translation: Come , chain me now [*] , and do listen to your son . Didn ’ t I tell you that you would discover what sort of person he is ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.857
sections.translation: What shall I do now ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.857b
sections.translation: Order me at once to be released , if you please ; for , if I ’ m not released , he ’ ll just now be overpowering the young man in our presence .

sections.label: Section 4.8.859
sections.original: Nihil est lucri quod me hodie facere mavelim ,
sections.translation: There ’ s no gain that I should this day take so much delight in making ,

sections.label: Section 4.8.860
sections.original: quam illum cubantem cum illa opprimere , ambo ut necem .
sections.translation: as I should in falling upon him as he reclines with her , so that I might kill them both .

sections.label: Section 4.8.861
sections.original: Audin quae loquitur ? quin tu me exsolvi iubes ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you hear what he says Why don ’ t you order me to be released ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.862
sections.translation: Unbind him . I ’ m ruined ; wretch that I am ! I ’ m in a dreadful fright .

sections.label: Section 4.8.863
sections.original: Tum illam , quae corpus publicat volgo suom ,
sections.translation: Then I ’ d make her , who publicly puts up her person for sale , not to say that she has got a person for her to laugh at .

sections.label: Section 4.8.865
sections.translation: You may make terms with him for a little money .

sections.label: Section 4.8.866b
sections.original: Pacisce ergo , obsecro , quid tibi lubet ,
sections.translation: Make terms , then , I beg , for what you like ; so that he mayn ’ t , in our presence , fall upon the young man , or kill him outright .

sections.label: Section 4.8.868
sections.original: Nunc nisi ducenti Philippi redduntur mihi ,
sections.translation: Unless the two hundred Philippeans are repaid to me at once , I ’ ll this instant swallow the lives of them both outright .

sections.label: Section 4.8.870
sections.original: Em illuc pacisce , si potes ; perge obsecro ,
sections.translation: Make terms with him , if you can ; prithee , do make haste ; agree for any sum you like .

sections.label: Section 4.8.871b
sections.translation: I ’ ll go , and do it carefully . ( Goes up to CLEOMACHUS .) Why are you exclaiming so ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.872b
sections.translation: Where ’ s your master ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.872c
sections.translation: Nowhere ; I don ’ t know . Do you wish the two hundred pieces to be promised you at once , so as to make no riot or disturbance here ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.875
sections.translation: There ’ s nothing that I would desire more .

sections.label: Section 4.8.875b
sections.original: Atque ut tibi mala multa ingeram ?
sections.translation: And that I may heap many a curse upon you ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.876
sections.translation: Just as you please .

sections.label: Section 4.8.876b
sections.translation: How the villain does cringe .

sections.label: Section 4.8.877
sections.original: Pater hic Mnesilochi est ; sequere , is promittet tibi .
sections.translation: ( To CLEOMACHUS .) This is the father of Mnesilochus : follow me ; he shall promise it you . Do you ask for the gold . As to the rest , a word ’ s enough [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.8.879
sections.translation: What has been done ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.879b
sections.translation: I ’ ve struck the bargain for two hundred Philippeans .

sections.label: Section 4.8.879c
sections.translation: Well done ! Goddess Salvation [*] ,

sections.label: Section 4.8.880
sections.original: mea , servavisti me . quam mox dico dabo ?
sections.translation: thou hast saved me . Well , how soon am I to say I ’ ll pay it ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.881
sections.original: Roga hunc tu , tu promitte huic .
sections.translation: Do you ask it of him ; and ( to NICOBULUS ) do you promise it him .

sections.label: Section 4.8.881b
sections.translation: I promise it . ( To CLEOMACHUS .) Come , ask me .

sections.label: Section 4.8.882
sections.translation: Will you give me two hundred golden Philippean pieces , lawful money ?
